author: Infused
text:
bl plugin policy/show: per-event §11 policy

bl-32e5 shipped plugin enable/disable/list but deliberately left the participant block read-only — SPEC §11 per-event policy (required/best-effort/gating) was still hand-edited JSON with validation deferred to the next config load.

`bl plugin policy <name> <event>=<kind> ...` upserts per-event policy; `--rm` drops one subscription, `--clear` removes the block (legacy sync_on_change fallback), `--no-legacy` writes an explicit empty map. This makes the None-vs-empty-{} loader distinction — legacy fallthrough vs subscribe-to-nothing — expressible without hand-editing JSON. `bl plugin show <name>` prints the resolved per-event policy.

Validation reuses Config::validate at the command boundary, so the SPEC §6.2 drop-is-observe-only rule and unknown event/kind tokens fail at command time, not at next load. Writes target the effective config only (the hub config under master_url); there is deliberately no per-clone --local surface — a federated clone overriding hub plugin policy is the exact drift the master-wins model prevents. `enable --sync-on-change` is now deprecated in favour of explicit policy.
